name: Dual Q-Table with Fixed Exponential-Decay Arbitration
description: Algebraic dual-system reinforcement learning agent maintaining two separate Q-tables: a model-free (habitual) Q-table updated by TD(0) and a model-based (goal-directed) Q-table computed prospectively from a learned transition model and hunger-modulated outcome desirability. The two value streams are blended via a deterministic arbitration weight that decays exponentially with state visit count, implementing the overtraining-to-habit shift (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008; postulate P4). Early in training, goal-directed control dominates; after extensive exposure, habitual cached values take over. Outcome devaluation (e.g., satiation reducing hunger) immediately affects model-based values but not model-free values, reproducing the key behavioral signature of goal-directed vs. habitual control (postulate P5).

variables.name: state
variables.description: Agent's current grid cell encoded as (x, y) tuple

variables.name: model_free_q_table
variables.description: Cached habitual action values. Dictionary mapping (state, action) pairs to scalar values, updated incrementally by TD(0) prediction errors. Insensitive to outcome desirability changes (postulate P3).

variables.name: model_based_q_value
variables.description: Goal-directed action values computed prospectively each step from the learned transition model and current hunger-modulated desirability. Not cached — recomputed every decision step (postulate P2).

variables.name: net_action_value
variables.description: Arbitrated blend of Q_MF and Q_MB, weighted by omega. Used for final action selection via softmax.

variables.name: hunger_drive
variables.description: Internal physiological state representing current need. Rises each step without eating, drops upon consuming food. Modulates goal-directed outcome desirability r_D (postulate P2, assumption: state-dependent desirability).

variables.name: decision_time_desirability
variables.description: Current subjective value of each possible outcome, modulated by hunger h. For food outcomes: r_D = h * r_food. For non-food outcomes: r_D = c_step.

variables.name: learned_transition_model
variables.description: Agent's estimate of action-outcome contingency p_hat(o|a,s). Dictionary mapping (state, action, outcome) triples to probabilities. Core element of goal-directed system (postulate P2).

variables.name: td_prediction_error
variables.description: Temporal difference prediction error: delta = r_received + gamma * max_a' Q_MF(s', a') - Q_MF(s, a). Drives model-free learning (Rangel et al., 2008).

variables.name: arbitration_weight
variables.description: Proportion of goal-directed control in the value mixture. Computed as exp(-lambda * N(s)), decaying from 1.0 (full GD) toward 0.0 (full habit) as state visit count increases (postulate P4).

variables.name: visit_count
variables.description: Number of times the agent has visited each state s. Dictionary mapping states to integer counts. Primary driver of the arbitration weight decay.

parameters.name: transition_model_learning_rate
parameters.source: Higher than alpha_MF — model-based systems update faster (Rangel, 2013; postulate P2)

parameters.name: habit_formation_rate
parameters.source: Controls speed of exponential decay of omega; tuned to match overtraining timeline (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008; postulate P4)

parameters.name: hunger_rise_rate
parameters.source: Per-step increase when not eating; biologically motivated hunger accumulation

parameters.name: satiation_amount
parameters.source: Hunger reduction upon eating; provides meaningful satiation per food event

parameters.name: base_food_reward
parameters.source: Normalized reward unit matching env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0

parameters.name: step_cost
parameters.source: Small penalty to encourage efficiency and discourage aimless wandering

rules.description: Hunger dynamics: hunger rises each step by eta and drops by phi when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: state-dependent desirability; internal hunger modulates goal-directed valuation

rules.description: Decision-time desirability: subjective value of food outcome is hunger-modulated; non-food outcome incurs step cost.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — goal-directed value uses current desirability r^D(o|s)

rules.description: Model-based Q-value computation (prospective). For eat action at food location: sum over outcomes of p_hat(o|eat,s) * r_D(o). For move actions: c_step + gamma * max_a' Q_MF(s', a') where s' is the destination cell. For eat with no food: c_step. For stay: c_step.
Transition model update: after observing outcome o_obs from action a in state s, update p_hat toward the observed outcome using learning rate alpha_T.
rules.source_postulate: P2 — action-outcome contingency p(o|a,s) is learned and updated from experience

rules.description: TD prediction error: computed after observing reward r_received and transitioning to new state s'.
rules.pseudocode: delta = r_received + gamma * max(Q_MF[s_next][a_prime] for a_prime in actions) - Q_MF[s][a]
rules.source_postulate: P3 — habitual system uses TD learning; prediction error delta drives cached value updates

rules.description: Model-free Q-value update: incremental TD(0) update using the prediction error.
rules.source_postulate: P3 — V_H(a|s) updated via incremental experience, not decomposed into contingency and desirability

rules.description: Arbitration weight: exponential decay based on state visit count. omega=1 at zero visits (full goal-directed), decays toward 0 (full habitual) with training.
rules.source_postulate: P4 — goal-directed control predominates early; habitual control predominates after extensive training

rules.description: Net action value: linear blend of model-based and model-free Q-values weighted by arbitration parameter omega.
rules.source_postulate: P1 — two dissociable valuation systems compete/cooperate via mixing

rules.description: Softmax action selection: probability of each action proportional to exponentiated net Q-value, controlled by inverse temperature beta.
rules.pseudocode:
probs = softmax(beta * Q_net[s][a] for a in actions)
selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)
rules.source_postulate: Assumption: common currency of value enters common action-selection mechanism

rules.description: Visit count increment: after each step in state s, increment the visit counter.
rules.source_postulate: P4 — training trial count is primary driver of habit formation

decision_logic.description: Each step: (1) Read current state s=(x,y), list of food resources, hunger h. (2) Compute hunger-modulated desirability r_D for food vs non-food outcomes (R2). (3) For each action, compute model-based Q_MB prospectively using learned transition model and r_D (R3). (4) Look up cached model-free Q_MF values for current state (R6 applied in prior steps). (5) Compute arbitration weight omega from visit count N(s) via exponential decay (R7). (6) Blend into Q_net = omega*Q_MB + (1-omega)*Q_MF (R8). (7) Select action via softmax over Q_net (R9). (8) After execution: update hunger (R1), transition model (R4), compute TD error (R5), update Q_MF (R6), increment N(s) (R10).
pseudocode: food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception.resources.get('food', [])]
# R3: Compute model-based Q for each action
Q_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(Q_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)
else:  # stay or eat with no food
# R7: Arbitration weight from visit count
Q_net[a] = omega * Q_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega) * Q_MF.get((s, a), 0.0)
probs = softmax([beta * Q_net[a] for a in actions])
r_received = r_food if ate_food else c_step
p_hat[(s, action, o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5))
for o in outcomes where o != o_obs:
p_hat[(s, action, o)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5))
# R5-R6: TD update for model-free Q
delta = r_received + gamma * max(Q_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions) - Q_MF.get((s, action), 0.0)
Q_MF[(s, action)] = Q_MF.get((s, action), 0.0) + alpha_MF * delta

perception_to_variables.food_positions: perception.resources.food — list of {x, y, type, palatability} dicts
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_position: any(f['x'] == perception.x and f['y'] == perception.y for f in perception.resources.get('food', []))
perception_to_variables.ate_food: action was 'eat' and perception.last_action_result indicates consumption

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action at food location → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions → internal step cost c_step = -0.01

expected_behaviors.description: Goal-directed dominance in novel states: when visiting a state for the first time (N(s)=0), omega=1.0 so the agent relies entirely on model-based prospective computation, choosing actions that lead toward food based on hunger-modulated desirability.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Place agent in new state with food nearby → assert omega == 1.0 → assert agent moves toward food when hungry

expected_behaviors.description: Habit formation via overtraining: after many visits to the same state, omega decays toward 0 and the agent's behavior is dominated by cached Q_MF values regardless of current hunger level.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run agent for 200 steps in same area → assert omega < 0.1 for frequently visited states → assert Q_net approximately equals Q_MF

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation sensitivity (early training): when the agent is sated (h≈0) and in a novel state (high omega), it should avoid eating because Q_MB reflects the low current desirability of food.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.01, N(s)=0 → compute Q_MB for eat → assert Q_MB_eat ≈ 0.01 (very low) → assert agent prefers non-eat actions

expected_behaviors.description: Devaluation insensitivity (late training): when the agent is sated but in an overtrained state (low omega), it may still eat because Q_MF reflects historically high rewards from eating.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.01, N(s)=100 → assert omega < 0.01 → Q_net dominated by Q_MF → if Q_MF[eat] was high from past experience, agent still eats

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger drives food-seeking: with high hunger (h≈1.0) and food available, the goal-directed system assigns high value to eating, producing directed movement toward food or immediate eating.
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set h=0.9, food at current position → assert Q_MB[eat] ≈ 0.9 → assert eat is most probable action

references: Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9, 545–556.
Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724.
